full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> When I first heard of this unique service,<|3.50|> <|3.50|> I didn't quite know what to make of it.<|6.34|> <|6.34|> For I had not grown up with this tradition in my small congregational church.<|12.50|> <|12.50|> My family is not of Scottish origin,<|15.50|> <|15.50|> so how might one understand the dress, bagpipes, dance, and blessings of this day?<|22.10|> <|22.10|> What does it all mean? Why are we all here?<|26.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The turning point came in my faith when I heard the call to broaden my horizons and seek out new ways of honoring God and celebrating the heritage of others, spending time overseas in Jerusalem and on to Nazareth and Galilee.<|18.78|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Naturally, by design, this faith leads me to you, to today, to this moment,<|9.00|> <|9.00|> to experience the beautiful liturgy, music, and traditions of the Kirkinola Tartans.<|16.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> St. Anselm describes this path for Christians as faith-seeking understanding.<|9.00|> <|9.00|> And it is in this space of curiosity and wonder that I this service take us back<|4.04|> <|4.04|> to 17th century Scotland,<|6.48|> <|7.46|> when after the Scots defeat at the Battle of Culloden,<|11.32|> <|11.32|> the Duke of Chamberlain passed the 1746 <|9.08|> and force the defeated Scots to assimilate into English culture and national identity.<|16.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What resulted instead was a clandestine effort by the Highlanders, they're called, to hold on to their identity through hiding pieces of tartan cloth under their garments and having them blessed during church services.<|18.30|><|endoftext|> peoplehood.<|10.94|> <|11.94|> They did not lose who they are as God's beloved children.<|15.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> time.<|1.80|><|endoftext|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> In the decades that followed these events, that discriminatory law was repealed and the long journey of healing began.<|11.00|> <|11.00|> Our nation here in this land, the United States of America, was born, along with our Episcopal Church.<|19.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The war for independence in America led Anglican Presbyterian<|7.08|> <|7.08|> priest named the Reverend Dr. Peter Marshall delivered a sermon titled "Kirk and O' the<|12.50|> <|12.50|> Tartan" that we come closer to where we are today.<|18.76|> be translated as the "churching" or "blessing" of the family,<|8.00|> <|8.00|> the blessing of the cloths, the blessing of the people.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> It is a beautiful tradition which we feel so blessed to host here at St. John's Cathedral<|21.00|> <|21.00|> in partnership with you and the Society of St. Andrew.<|25.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> This blessing is an Easter place in which Christians are called into,<|7.00|> <|7.00|> to hear the voice of the Good Shepherd and follow where he leads,<|12.00|> <|12.00|> to celebrate one's own culture, history, and practices,<|17.00|> <|17.00|> holding space and engaging with people of other backgrounds.<|22.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> a shared commitment to community and family that <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The love present here in Christ,<|5.34|> <|5.34|> the love Scottish-American people have in their identity,<|10.88|> <|10.88|> and the church's blessing are profound examples of the power of Easter,<|16.76|> <|16.76|> a triumph of life over the forces of death,<|21.86|> <|21.86|> the message of good news in a risen Lord,<|27.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> With this love we share here today, this joy, this music, pride, worship and dance, may we ever be empowered to share it with others.<|14.34|> <|15.16|> To share the blessing of a new Jerusalem.<|17.42|> <|18.50|> The blessing of God Almighty.<|20.08|> <|21.18|> The blessing of the whole human family with all.<|24.96|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.96|><|endoftext|>
